diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response and acute on chronic decompensated congestive heart failure
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with worsening shortness of breath and clinical findings consistent with atrial fibrillation and fluid overload. Initial assessment demonstrated tachycardia, elevated jugular venous pressure, and bibasilar crackles, with chest X-ray showing cardiomegaly and pleural effusions, indicating heart failure exacerbation. Management included fluid removal and cardioversion, which resulted in improved symptoms.
